stats_fallback.christened: 2017-12-21 (PortMiami Terminal B — MSC's first ship christening in North America; godmother Sophia Loren, host Mario Lopez, live performances by Andrea Bocelli with Voices of Haiti children's choir and Ricky Martin)
stats_fallback.yacht_club_status: Installed from 2017 delivery. 86 suites spanning Decks 16, 18, 19 with Concierge Area and two-story Top Sail Lounge (Deck 16), MSC Yacht Club Restaurant and MSC Yacht Club Grill (Deck 18), 1,600 m² MSC Yacht Club Pool with two whirlpools (Deck 19), and MSC Yacht Club Sundeck & Bar (Deck 19). The Yacht Club concept had already debuted on the Fantasia-class ships before MSC Seaside — Seaside was the first Seaside-class ship to carry it from build, not MSC's first Yacht Club overall.

related.class_notes: Seaside Class. MSC Seaside is the lead ship; her only direct sister is MSC Seaview (2018). The enlarged MSC Seashore (2021) and MSC Seascape (2022) form the separate Seaside EVO sub-class (170,412 GT vs 153,516 GT; 339 m vs 323 m; 2,270 cabins vs 2,066; 4 MDRs vs 2). The EVOs are derivatives, not direct sisters. Seaside's distinctive dining signature is the Asian Market Kitchen by Roy Yamaguchi chef collaboration (Pan-Asian, sushi, teppanyaki trio), which is unique to the original Seaside pair and not found on the EVOs.

deployment.spring_2026_mediterranean: Early 2026 Mediterranean season from Barcelona calling at La Goulette, Palermo, Naples, Livorno, and Marseille.
deployment.oct_2026_transatlantic: 14-night transatlantic repositioning from Barcelona to Miami in October 2026 via Funchal and San Juan.
deployment.apr_dec_2026_bahamas: 13 April - 14 December 2026: year-round 3- and 4-night Bahamas round-trips from PortMiami to Nassau and MSC Ocean Cay Marine Reserve (Friday and Monday turnarounds).
deployment.current_ais: 11 April 2026: NW Atlantic Ocean en route from Miami (departed Apr 10 22:36 UTC) to Ocean Cay, speed ~7.5 kn, draft 8.5 m.

history.christened: 21 December 2017, PortMiami Terminal B (Sophia Loren; MSC's first ship christening in North America; hosted by Mario Lopez; performances by Andrea Bocelli with Voices of Haiti children's choir and Ricky Martin)
history.2022_palermo_drydock: July 2022 unscheduled drydock at Fincantieri Palermo for electrical / engine issue; returned to service 19 July 2022
history.2026_mooring_incident: 5 February 2026 — brief mooring incident at MSC Ocean Cay: sudden wind gusts (29-37 mph) snapped mooring lines, bow drifted from pier while passengers were ashore, stern remained secured. Bridge team used bow thrusters to reposition. No injuries, no significant structural damage. Departed Ocean Cay on schedule and returned to Miami 6 February 2026.

discrepancies_flagged.imo_contamination_avoided: Initial guess of IMO 9732757 was WRONG — that IMO does not match MSC Seaside. Correct IMO is 9745366. Sixth IMO correction caught in the MSC fleet repair (after Bellissima, Euribia, Magnifica, Meraviglia, Musica/Orchestra swap, Seascape).
discrepancies_flagged.gt_typo: Maritime Executive article cites '153,316' GT — a typo. MSC, Fincantieri, and AIS all agree on 153,516 GT.
discrepancies_flagged.delivery_date: Wikipedia says 29 November 2017; some MSC press releases say 30 November 2017. Both likely reflect handover vs ceremony date. Use 29 November per Wikipedia.
discrepancies_flagged.zipline_length: Sometimes reported as 360 ft in secondary sources. Correct per MSC Technical Sheet: TWO separate ziplines of 120 m each (~394 ft each).
discrepancies_flagged.buffet_naming: The 2017 launch-era dining factsheet called the second buffet 'Colonial Buffet & Pizzeria'. Current MSC Technical Sheet renames it 'Biscayne Bay Restaurant & Buffet'. Use Biscayne Bay for current page.
discrepancies_flagged.sisters: Some sources list MSC Seashore and/or MSC Seascape as 'sisters'. Strictly speaking Seaside's only direct sister is MSC Seaview — Seashore and Seascape form the separate Seaside EVO sub-class.
discrepancies_flagged.max_capacity: MSC tech sheet says 5,336 max; Fincantieri page gives 6,592 as 'maximum persons on board' (includes crew + total souls). Cruise Critic cites 4,140 lower berth. Three different metrics; use 4,140 double / 5,336 max passengers.
discrepancies_flagged.asian_market_kitchen_chef: The chef collaboration is with Roy Yamaguchi and is unique to the original Seaside pair (Seaside + Seaview). Not on Seashore or Seascape.
